_comment: Copy this file to profile.json and fill in your own skills and preferences. profile.json is gitignored and must never be committed.
_primary_skills_help: primary_skills is an array of objects. Each object has: 'description' (string — the skill name), 'years_active' (integer — years of experience), 'active' (boolean — true if currently using this skill, false if dormant/historical). Active skills are weighted more heavily in LLM scoring.
anti_preferences: no pure frontend roles (React / Vue / Angular only — no backend component)
no QA or test engineer roles
no pure DevOps or sysadmin roles with no software development scope
no junior or associate level positions
_education_help: education is an array of free-text strings, one entry per degree or credential. Each entry is injected into the LLM scoring prompt so the model does not flag degree requirements as missing when the candidate already satisfies them. Format each entry however is natural — e.g. 'B.S. Computer Science, State University, 2012'.
_location_help: Optional nested block. 'center' is a geocodable string used as the geospatial filter origin (uses OpenStreetMap Nominatim — no API key needed). 'radius_km' is the hard filter radius; listings outside it are discarded before LLM scoring. 'geocode_fallback' controls what happens when a listing location cannot be geocoded: 'pass' (default) lets it through, 'discard' rejects it. 'notes' is free-text injected into the LLM scoring prompt; if absent, a description is auto-generated from center + radius_km.

location.notes: Open to remote or on-site / hybrid in South Florida

_scoring_notes_help: scoring_notes is injected verbatim into the LLM scoring prompt. Use it to give the model hard guidance that overrides or supplements the other fields — e.g. seniority weighting rules, industries to deprioritise, or any other scoring policy. Accepts a string or an array of strings.
scoring_notes: Senior-level roles are strongly preferred. If two roles are otherwise equal, a Senior role should score 1-2 points higher than a Mid-level role.
Mid-level roles are acceptable and should not be excluded, but must be noted in concerns.
A Mid-level role with excellent technical fit can still score 7-8. A Senior role with weaker fit should not automatically outscore it.
